nodes.label: Node.js → Frontend JSON Contract
nodes.rationale: Invariant contract: {breed: string, confidence: string (e.g. '91%'), care: {exercise, grooming, temperament}}. Node.js owns all transformations (snake_case→display name, float→percent, care enrichment) so Python stays minimal and stateless.

nodes.label: FastAPI Lifespan — Model Load on Startup
nodes.norm_label: fastapi lifespan — model load on startup

nodes.label: Python/Node Responsibility Boundary
nodes.rationale: Python (predict.py) is strictly responsible for inference only: load model, preprocess image, run inference, return raw breed name and confidence float. All display formatting, care data enrichment, and frontend contract assembly belong to Node.js/aiService.js.

nodes.label: Two-Phase Transfer Learning Strategy
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 freezes MobileNetV2 base, trains only classification head with LR=1e-3. Phase 2 unfreezes layers from index 100+ and fine-tunes with very low LR=1e-5 to specialize in dog breeds without destroying pretrained ImageNet weights.

nodes.label: buildCard — Build History Entry DOM Card
nodes.norm_label: buildcard — build history entry dom card

nodes.label: claude.md — Development Rules for Claude Code
nodes.norm_label: claude.md — development rules for claude code

nodes.label: Rationale: FastAPI Chosen Over TensorFlow.js
nodes.rationale: FastAPI was chosen over TensorFlow.js because: (1) model weight (~10MB) would be downloaded by each user; (2) SavedModel→TFLite→TFJS conversion introduces preprocessing inconsistencies; (3) isolating the model in Python allows updating it without touching Node.js or frontend; (4) Python training toolchain (Keras, sklearn, matplotlib) lives natively in Python.

nodes.label: Rationale: JavaScript Vanilla Without Framework
nodes.rationale: Project spec explicitly forbids React/Vue/Angular. The IIFE module pattern (history.js, stats.js) provides encapsulation and public API without a build step, making the stack minimal and portable for an MVP.

nodes.label: Rationale: Separate Node.js from Python
nodes.rationale: Node.js handles file I/O, request orchestration, and JSON transformation. Python handles pure ML inference. This separation means the ML engine (TensorFlow, model format, architecture) can be replaced without modifying a single line of Node.js or frontend code.

nodes.label: Rationale: Invariant JSON Contract from Node.js to Frontend
nodes.rationale: Python returns minimal representation (snake_case breed, raw float confidence). Node.js is solely responsible for transformation (snake_case→Title Case, float→percentage string, enrichment with care data from breeds.js). This shields the frontend from any internal model changes.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: invariant json contract from node.js to frontend

nodes.label: Rationale: Separate localStorage Keys for History and Stats
nodes.rationale: History keeps only the last 5 entries (MAX_ENTRIES). Stats use a separate key to accumulate totalCount, breedCounts, and confidenceSum across ALL analyses so the dashboard reflects the true running totals, not just the last 5.
nodes.norm_label: rationale: separate localstorage keys for history and stats

nodes.label: Two-Phase Transfer Learning Strategy
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 (Feature Extraction): MobileNetV2 base frozen, only classification head trained (164K params), Adam lr=1e-3, up to 20 epochs. Phase 2 (Fine-Tuning): layers from index 100+ unfrozen, Adam lr=1e-5 (100x smaller to preserve pretrained weights), only runs if Phase 1 val_accuracy >= 60%. This two-phase approach avoids destroying ImageNet representations while specializing in canine features.

nodes.label: Preprocessing Must Be Identical in Training and Inference
nodes.rationale: Pillow open→RGB→resize(224,224,LANCZOS)→float32/255.0→expand_dims must be identical between prepare_dataset.py/train.py and predict.py. If the model was trained with [0,1] normalization but inference passes [0,255], the model sees completely different distributions and produces nonsense predictions. This is one of the most common ML deployment errors.
nodes.norm_label: preprocessing must be identical in training and inference

nodes.label: IIFE Module Pattern for Frontend Encapsulation
nodes.rationale: history.js and stats.js use the IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression) pattern to simulate modules with explicit public APIs ({ save, clear, render } and { record, clear, render }) without requiring a bundler or build step. The modules are completely autonomous — they do not know about the form or the analysis flow in main.js.
nodes.norm_label: iife module pattern for frontend encapsulation
